postBody: Performance testing ensures your application handles expected user load
Always use CSV data sets for parameterization to simulate real users
API endpoints must be validated under concurrent user scenarios
Stress tests reveal breaking points and help plan infrastructure scaling
Spike tests simulate sudden traffic bursts like flash sales or viral events
Analyzing percentiles helps identify slow transactions under load
Configuring proper ramp-up periods simulates realistic user arrival rates
Correlation extracts dynamic values from responses for use in subsequent requests
APDEX quantifies user satisfaction based on response time thresholds
High throughput with low error rate indicates a healthy performing API
Integrate JMeter into CI pipelines to enforce performance thresholds
P95 and P99 percentiles show the worst case experience for your users
Running JMeter in non-GUI mode is recommended for production test execution
JMeter HTML dashboards provide comprehensive visual performance insights
Endurance tests detect memory leaks and performance degradation over time
Thread groups define how many virtual users execute requests simultaneously
Centralizing base URL in HTTP defaults simplifies test plan maintenance
JSON path extractors are essential for correlating dynamic API responses
Good reporting includes throughput graphs error rates and response percentiles
Response assertions validate functional correctness during performance runs
